description: Inside a well-lit office, Rachel stands near the door wearing a dark pantsuit. Chandler sits behind a desk wearing a blue button-down shirt and dark socks, with his legs bare. He says, "Could you be a doll and grab them and scoot on over here and unlock me?"
Rachel remains by the door, her lips pressed together, maintaining her gaze on Chandler.
Chandler gestures slightly with his left hand and says, "And on a totally separate subject, that is a lovely pantsuit." Rachel turns her head slightly.
Rachel continues staring at Chandler, her arms resting at her sides.
Rachel gestures toward Chandler and says, "You promised you would break up with her."
Chandler leans his head forward and replies, "I did break up with her. She just took it really, really well."
Rachel inhales, raises both hands to her chest level, and steps further into the room toward the desk.
Rachel leans forward, lowering her hands toward the desk, and asks, "That you were jeopardizing my career never entered your mind?"
Chandler sits up straighter and replies, "It did enter my mind. But then something happened that made it"
Chandler continues, "shoot right out," looking up at Rachel.
Rachel looks down briefly, shaking her head.
Rachel raises one hand, palm open, and says, "You know what, Chandler?"
Rachel points toward him and turns away, saying, "You got yourself into those cuffs you get yourself out of." Chandler raises his voice, replying, "No, no."
Chandler gestures with his open left hand and says, "I can't get myself right out of them. You must have me confused with the Amazing Chandler."
Rachel stops near the door, keeping her back mostly toward him, then turns her head back.
Chandler leans forward, his pitch rising as he says, "Come on, you have to unlock me. She could be gone for hours. And I'm cold. And"
Chandler glances toward a mannequin in the corner. Rachel exhales sharply, raising both hands to shoulder height.
Rachel steps away from the door and says, "Chandler?"
Rachel walks toward the desk, holding up a small key. She says, "All right, it."
Rachel points the key at him and says, "You never see Joanna again." Chandler shakes his head and replies, "Never."
Rachel maintains eye contact, pointing the key, and says, "You never come into this office again."
Chandler says, "Fine." Rachel lowers her hand and says, "You give me back my Walkman."
Chandler pauses, his mouth slightly open as he looks at her.
Chandler shakes his head slightly and says, "I never borrowed your Walkman."
Rachel leans forward slightly, keeping her gaze on him.
Rachel says, "Well, then I lost it. You buy me one you got. Here we go. Come on. This is great." She leans down over the desk, inserting the key into the handcuff on Chandler's right wrist.
Rachel pulls the unlocked cuff away. Chandler immediately grabs his right wrist with his left hand, rubbing it as Rachel stands up straight.
Rachel looks at him and asks, "Does it hurt?" Chandler stops rubbing his wrist and replies, "No, I just always see guys doing this when they get handcuffs taken off."
Rachel turns away. Chandler stands up, steps away from the desk, and pulls his pants off a hook on the wall.
Chandler holds his pants up in front of his body and says, "Hello, sweet pinks."
Rachel turns back to face Chandler, raising one hand.
Rachel says, "Wait a minute. What are you going to tell Joanna?"

Chandler turns to her, holding his pants, and asks, "About what?" Rachel steps forward, gesturing with one hand, and says, "When she sees that you're gone, she's going to know that I let you out and that I was in here and I'm"
Rachel continues, "going to get fired." Chandler replies, "I'll make something up. I'm good at lying. I actually did borrow your Walkman."
Rachel steps toward him, reaching her hands forward, and says, "No, there's nothing to make up. She's going to know that I have a key to her office."
Rachel grabs his arm and says, "I've got to get you locked back the way you were. I"
Chandler pulls his arm away and steps back, saying, "don't think so."
Rachel lunges forward, grabbing Chandler's arm. Chandler pulls back, hopping backward on one leg while keeping his pants pressed against his waist with his other hand.
Rachel shoves Chandler toward a tall filing cabinet near the door. Chandler flails his arms, trying to resist as Rachel forces his right arm toward the cabinet handle.
Rachel fastens the handcuff onto Chandler's right wrist and the cabinet handle. She steps back quickly, leaving him secured to the cabinet.
Chandler looks at his handcuffed wrist and says, "Well, this is much better," his voice raised.
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Joey sits at a small table. The Salesman, wearing a suit and glasses, stands nearby holding a large book.
The Salesman flips a page in the book and says, "So here's somebody interesting."
The Salesman steps closer to Joey and asks, "Joey, what do you know about Van Gogh?"
Joey rests his hands on the table, looking at the Salesman.
Joey gestures briefly and says, "He cut off his ear." The Salesman leans in and asks, "And?" Joey shrugs and says, "I'm out."
The Salesman rotates the book and places it flat on the table in front of Joey.
Joey looks down at the open book and says, "He painted that? Wow, that's pretty nice."
Joey looks up at the Salesman and says, "I thought he cut off his ear because he sucked."
Joey gestures toward the book and asks, "What else you got in there? Let's see."
The Salesman picks up the book and holds it open, looking down at the pages.
The Salesman looks at Joey and asks, "Where does the Pope live?" Joey immediately answers, "In the woods."
The Salesman stares silently at Joey. Joey furrows his brows, maintaining eye contact.
Joey raises one hand, his eyes widening, and says, "No, wait, that's the joke answer."
Joey rests his hand briefly against his forehead.
Joey lowers his hand, looks at the Salesman, and says, "Actually, it's Vatican City."
The Salesman flips a page in the book and asks, "Now, what do you know about vulcanized rubber?"
Joey smiles, points at the Salesman, and answers, "Spock's birth control."
The Salesman throws his head back and laughs loudly, his shoulders shaking. Joey grins at him.
The Salesman stops laughing, points down at Joey, and says loudly, "You need these books."
The Salesman smiles and looks back down at the book. On-screen text reading "EXECUTIVE PRODUCERS KEVIN S. BRIGHT MARTA KAUFFMAN" appears over the scene.
In a bedroom, Monica lies on her back on a home gym machine, her hands gripping its vertical bars.
Phoebe enters the room and says, "Hey."
Monica replies, "Hi." Phoebe steps closer, gesturing to the room, and asks, "This used to be your room?"
Phoebe looks at the exercise equipment.
Phoebe says, "Wow."
Phoebe turns her gaze down toward Monica.
Phoebe says, "You must have been in really good shape as a kid."
Monica remains lying flat on the machine.
Monica releases the bars, sits up quickly, and says, "Oh, I'm such an idiot."
Monica gestures with both hands, her volume rising as she says, "I can't believe I actually thought she could change."
Phoebe walks over and sits on the edge of the gym machine bench next to Monica. "Look, who cares what your mom thinks?"
Phoebe points a finger at Monica and says, "So you pulled a Monica."
Monica lowers her gaze and remains silent.
Monica looks down and says, "Good. I'm glad that's catching on."
Phoebe gestures with her right hand, responding, "But why does that have to be a bad thing? Just change what it means. Go down"
Phoebe maintains her gaze on Monica.
Phoebe continues, "there and prove your mother wrong."
Phoebe gestures with both hands. "You finished the job you were hired to do. And we'll call that pulling a Monica."
Monica looks at Phoebe.
Monica furrows her eyebrows and asks, "What?"
Phoebe replies, "Okay, if a kid gets straight A's, his parents would say, yeah, you pulled a Monica."
Monica listens, her expression fixed.
Phoebe continues, "Or A fireman saves a baby. And they go, yeah. No, he pulled a Monica."
Monica continues to listen.
Phoebe gestures broadly again, "Or someone hits a home run and the announcer says, yeah, that one's out of here. Because some things don't change."
Monica nods slightly.
Monica stands up, her speaking pace faster. "Right. I'll go down there but I'm not going to serve the lasagna. I'm going to serve something that I make."
Monica turns and walks out of the room holding a jacket. Phoebe stands, steps over to the gym machine, and sits on the bench where Monica was lying.
Phoebe grips the two vertical bars and says, "Oh,"
Phoebe pulls the vertical bars toward her chest.
Holding the bars together, Phoebe says, "wow."
Phoebe releases the bars.
Phoebe smiles and says, "My breasts are really strong."
The setting changes to a daytime exterior shot of the New York City skyline featuring the Twin Towers. It then changes to an office interior.

Rachel pulls backward on a heavy filing cabinet drawer, dragging it across the floor, and says, "You." Chandler, wearing only a blue dress shirt, dark socks, and underwear, walks alongside the drawer with his left wrist handcuffed to its handle.
Rachel continues pulling the heavy drawer across the floor as Chandler follows.
Rachel pulls at the drawer and says rapidly, "Chandler. Chandler, please. I have to get you locked up back the way you were. I'm so gonna lose my job."
Rachel releases the drawer and steps back.
Rachel turns her back to Chandler, grasping her own lapels, and says, "She's very private about her office."
Chandler watches her.
Chandler extends his free right arm outward and says, "Now I know why."
Chandler looks around the office.
Chandler points his finger at Rachel and says, "Look, you were in trouble either way, okay? If she comes in and sees me locked to this instead of the chair she's going to"
Chandler continues speaking, "know you were in here. So you might as well just let me go."
Rachel stares at Chandler, her mouth slightly open.
Rachel extends her hand toward him, offering, "What if I clean your bathroom for a month?" Chandler replies quickly, "Still wouldn't be clean."
Chandler keeps his gaze on Rachel.
Chandler points his finger firmly at Rachel, stating, "All I want is my freedom."
Rachel presses her hands together.
Rachel gestures with her hands, offering, "Foot rubs for a month."
Chandler pulls the drawer toward the door and yells, "Freedom." Rachel follows closely, speaking rapidly, "I'll take all of your photos and I'll put them into photo albums."
Chandler continues to shout loudly over her, "Freedom. I want my freedom. Why would you hear me?"
Chandler steps into the doorway, shouting into the hallway, "Sophie, help me. Help me. Sophie."
A woman is briefly visible in the hallway before Rachel rushes over and pulls the door partially shut.
Rachel grabs a necktie from the desk, rushes to Chandler, and stuffs it into his mouth while shouting, "Give it."
Chandler stands with the necktie protruding from his mouth, his left arm still cuffed to the drawer.
Rachel raises both hands, palms facing him, and says, "Just calm down."
Chandler breathes heavily with the tie in his mouth, his eyes fixed on Rachel.
Chandler reaches up with his free right hand and pulls the necktie out of his mouth.
Chandler points his finger at Rachel and says, "I'm gonna say this for the last time."
Chandler raises his free hand near his face.

Shouting, Chandler says, "Would you please just" and swings his arm down. His cuffed left hand strikes the filing cabinet; the top drawer springs open, hitting him in the head. He collapses to the floor out of frame.

Rachel gasps and covers her mouth with both hands. The setting changes to an apartment living area. Joey sits on a lawn chair at a patio table, holding a large book, while the Salesman in a suit sits across from him.
Joey looks up from the book toward the Salesman and says, "well, there's a lot I didn't know about vomit."
Joey shakes the Salesman's hand and passes the book to him. A white duck walks into the background from the hallway.
The Salesman points toward the duck and says, "In a minute."
The Salesman turns his gaze back to Joey.
The Salesman gestures with his hand and asks, "So what do you say, Joey?"
The Salesman gestures expansively as he speaks. "You get the whole set of encyclopedias for one $200? That works out to just $50 a book. One"
Joey furrows his eyebrows and asks, "$200?"
Joey smiles slightly and points a finger at himself.
Joey asks, "You think I have one $200?"
Joey gestures toward his chest.
Joey gestures around the room, saying, "I'm home in the middle of the day and I got patio furniture in my living room."
The Salesman smiles and looks down at the book on the table.
The Salesman points at Joey and says, "I guess there's a few things you don't get from book learning."
The Salesman gestures with his hand and asks, "Well, what can you swing?"
Joey gestures with his right hand and replies, "How about zero down and zero a month for a long, long time?"
The Salesman gestures with both hands.
The Salesman asks, "You don't have anything?" Joey stands up, pulling at his pockets. "You want to see what I got? Okay," Joey replies.
Joey remains standing and searches his pockets.
Joey picks items from his right pocket and places them on the table. "I got a baby Tootsie Roll, a movie stub," he says.
Joey continues showing the items on his palm. "...keys, a Kleenex," he adds.
Joey reaches into his pocket again and pinches a small object.
Joey holds up the items. "a rock and an army man. Hey," he says, smiling as he looks at the army man.
The Salesman points at the table and raises his hand in a stopping motion.
The Salesman says, "okay, I get the picture. Thanks for your time," and stands up, lifting his briefcase.
Joey pulls a bill from his pocket and says, "And a 50." Looking at the bill, he adds, "These"
Joey looks down at his pants and finishes, "must be Chandler's pants." The Salesman stops and turns back around.
The Salesman holds up his briefcase and looks at Joey.
The Salesman points at Joey. "For $50, you can get one book. What'll it be?" he asks.
The Salesman waves his hand and lists, "A-B-C-"
Joey holds a black encyclopedia and points at it. "-I think I'm going to stick with the V. I want to see how this bad boy turns out," he says.
The Salesman smiles, chuckles, and puts the $50 bill in his breast pocket.

The setting changes to an outdoor view of a city street with yellow taxis passing under a bridge. The setting then changes to an office interior where Rachel points at Chandler. Chandler wears a blue dress shirt and no pants.
Rachel paces in front of Chandler. Leaning against a filing cabinet, Chandler says, "I will buy and wrap all of your Christmas gifts." Rachel shakes her head and yells, "No."
Rachel clenches her fists and shakes them near her waist. Chandler steps forward.
Chandler holds his hands up and says, "I'll squeeze you fresh orange juice every morning." Rachel turns toward him. "with extra pulp?" she asks.
Chandler nods and says, "Yeah." Rachel grabs her hair and screams, "No," her volume rising.
Rachel throws her hands down and paces away from Chandler. Chandler watches her, resting his hands on his waist.
Rachel stops pacing, turns to Chandler, and raises her hands. Chandler points at her and says, "I've got it." Rachel steps toward him and says, "You don't have it."
Rachel spreads her hands out wide.
Rachel keeps her arms open and says, "I've so got it."
Rachel points a finger at Chandler.
Pointing at Chandler, Rachel says, "There's going to be rumors about this. There's no way to stop it. Sophie knows."
Rachel continues, "Monica and Phoebe know." Chandler leans forward and asks, "How do Monica and Phoebe know?" Rachel gestures with her hand and replies, "Well, I called them"
Rachel points at Chandler.
Rachel says, "when they asked me what I saw." She then steps closer to him, pointing her finger upwards, and says, "I can be very generous"
Rachel lowers her hands and stares at Chandler, adjusting her posture.
Rachel smiles and says, "or very stingy," emphasizing the words.
Chandler stares back at Rachel, remaining leaning against the cabinet.
Chandler says, "Go on." Rachel smiles broadly and says, "I can make you a legend."
Rachel nods at Chandler.
Rachel gestures broadly with her hands and says, "I can make you this generation's"
Rachel stares at Chandler, keeping her expression steady as Chandler waits.
Rachel finishes, "Milton Berle." Chandler nods. Rachel adds, "Oh, not compared to you."
Chandler smiles and raises his eyebrows, resting his arm on the filing cabinet. The setting changes to a kitchen, where Phoebe carries a plate of food toward Monica.
Phoebe tells Monica, "Well, they're not even touching the lasagna." Monica, wearing a white chef's coat, turns and asks, "Really?"
Phoebe replies, "Oh, they love your casserole." Monica smiles and pumps her fists, whispering, "Yes."
Phoebe looks down at the plate and says, "Yes, it's hard to believe just a little while ago this was nothing but ingredients."
Phoebe sets the plate down. Judy enters the kitchen wearing a satin robe and approaches Monica.
Judy stops in front of Monica and says, "Everyone seems to like your dish."
Monica looks at Judy.
Monica asks, "And you?" keeping her gaze fixed on Judy.
Judy looks at Monica silently.
Judy says, "I thought it was"
Judy hesitates, her face neutral.
Judy finishes, "quite tasty."
Monica smiles widely.
Monica gestures with her hands as she says, "So if everyone liked it and you liked it that would make this a success."
Monica pauses briefly, then adds, "Which would make you a bitch," leaning forward slightly.
Monica's smile fades as she stops moving. Judy stares back, her mouth slightly open.
Monica waves her hands in front of her chest and stammers, "Well, I was going for wrong. But we can use your words."
Judy clasps her hands in front of her.
Judy nods slightly and says, "Yes. Well, I was wrong. And I have to say" speaking at a steady, soft pace.
Judy keeps her hands clasped and maintains eye contact with Monica.
Judy continues, "you really impressed me today." Monica smiles.
Monica says, "Wow," exhaling softly. Phoebe steps up behind Monica.
Monica chuckles softly as Phoebe puts a hand on Monica's shoulder and says, "Wow, you might even say that she pulled a Monica."
Judy looks at Phoebe.
Phoebe points at Monica and says, "She doesn't know we switched it," keeping her hand on Monica's shoulder.
Judy nods slowly, looking between Phoebe and Monica.
Judy looks at Monica, raises her finger slightly, and begins, "And the next time you cater for me,"
Judy finishes, "there'll be nothing but ice in the freezer." Monica's smile widens.
Monica places a hand on her chest.
Monica nods and says, "It really means a lot," her voice soft.
Judy looks down at her hands.
As Judy brings a finger to her mouth, Monica points at her and says, "Oh, and mom, don't bite your"
Judy stops biting her nail and lowers her hand, looking at Monica. The setting changes to the outside of a coffee shop window displaying the "Central Perk" logo.
Production credits appear on screen over brief images of Joey, Rachel, Chandler, Judy, Phoebe, and Monica, before the video concludes.
In a coffee shop, Phoebe and Monica sit on a sofa. Rachel rests on the armrest nearby, and Joey and Ross sit around a coffee table.
Chandler walks in. Multiple voices say, "hello. Hey."
Chandler stands behind the sofa.
Phoebe and Monica smile, looking toward him. "Hello Chandler," they say.
Chandler stands beside Rachel, looking down at her, while she rests her hand near her mouth.
Chandler leans his head down close to Rachel and says, "I love you."
Chandler straightens back up, smiling.
Joey gestures with a small wooden stirrer and asks, "Hey, what's going on?"
Joey, Ross, and Phoebe lean forward, their heads close together as Joey whispers.
Sitting back, Joey shakes his head and says, "No, he doesn't."
Ross laughs, holding an open magazine. Chandler looks at his wristwatch.
Chandler taps his watch and says, "2 hours that lasted."
Rachel raises one hand briefly.
"So did you break up with Joanna?" Rachel asks. Chandler replies, "I think so."
Joey points his stirrer and adds, "That's a good thing you got out when you did before she blew up like that. Vesuvius," expanding his hands outward on the phrase "blew up."
Ross turns to look at Joey.
"the volcano?" Ross asks.
Joey gestures with the stirrer, saying, "Yeah, and speaking of volcanoes, man, are they a violent, igneous rock formation."
Rachel frowns slightly, staring at Joey.
"What?" Rachel asks.
Joey widens his eyes and raises a hand, saying, "Yeah, lava spewing, hot ash. Of course some are dormant."
Phoebe and Monica stare silently at Joey.
"Why are you talking about volcanoes all of a sudden?" Phoebe asks.
Joey shrugs and says, "We can talk about something else. What do you want to talk about? Vivisection?"
Joey lowers the stirrer.
"The vas deferens? The Vietnam War?" Joey lists, pausing between the phrases.
Phoebe and Monica exchange a glance.
Joey leans forward slightly, asking, "Oh, did anybody see that documentary on the Korean War?"
Phoebe and Monica continue looking at him.
Ross lowers his magazine and says, "Korea is such a beautiful country with such a sad history."
Joey looks at Ross.
Chandler crosses his arms and says loudly, "Could there be more?"
"Kims?" Chandler finishes. Joey bursts into loud laughter.
The group smiles as Joey continues laughing. Production credits appear in white text over the scene.
Joey's laughter fades as he leans his head back against the sofa. The setting changes to an office interior. Rachel walks in holding a brown paper bag.
"Who's out there?" an off-screen voice asks. Rachel walks toward a desk, saying, "It's me. Good morning."
"Rachel, could you come in here a moment, please?" Joanna asks from behind a closed door.
"Yeah, sure. They didn't have poppy seed bagels, so I got sesame. Oh," Rachel replies. She opens the door and stops, her eyes widening.
"my word," Rachel finishes.
Joanna sits in an office chair wearing only a white slip, with one wrist handcuffed to the chair's armrest. Production credits continue on screen.
Gesturing with her free hand, Joanna says, "I seem to have had a slight office mishap."
Rachel stands still in the doorway, staring at Joanna.
Joanna points her free hand. "Could you please get the key off the back of the door for me?" she asks. "Yeah," Rachel says, turning.
Rachel drops the bag onto the desk, quickly closes the office door, and reaches for the keys hanging on it.
Rachel hurries to Joanna's side, leaning down and inserting the key into the handcuff.
"if you could," Joanna says.
"Oh, I'm sorry," Rachel replies, fumbling with the lock.
The handcuff clicks open. Rachel pulls the cuff away as Joanna brings her hands together, rubbing her freed wrist.
"You can tell your friend Chandler that we're definitely broken up this time," Joanna says. "Okay," Rachel replies.
Joanna continues holding her wrist. A copyright notice appears on screen, after which the video turns completely black.
The screen remains black.
